rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDUARDA O. BUTAC
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues (tentative rulings) have not been addressed since the initial hearing on 12/12/2022 and since the Court determined the persons entitled to distribution (who must receive notice of this interim accounting petition) on 07/24/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No original statements of the bank accounts listed on Schedule F have been filed. See LRSF 14.34(D). 3) A copy of the petition must be served on the person who requested special notice. Item 5 of the proof of service attached to the DE-120 form must be checked. See Probate Code section 1252(a). 4) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 4) Please file a copy of the closing escrow statement for the sale of the estate real property. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YOUNG JOON CHO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/25/2023 (no earlier date available) for the following additional issues to be addressed: 1) The attorney's signature and petitioner's verification must be removed from the proposed order (only the Court's signature block should be provided). 2) A legal description of the estate real property must be included in the body of the proposed order (no attachment or exhibit is allowed). 3) The probate referee's commission ($1,771) must be paid before the Court can approve final distribution. Please file a copy of a receipt or other proof of payment. 4) An allegation must be made that the petitioner has advanced sufficient cash (including a refund from the attorney) to the estate for payment of the attorneys' fees and costs and is not seeking reimbursement from the other beneficiary's share of the estate. Note: Each attorney's "retainer" can only be used to pay the costs (but not fees) without prior court approval. The retainer balance, if any, must be refunded to the petitioner so that payment of the attorneys' fees can be ordered to be made out of the estate, as required by Probate Code section 10831(d).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUSSELL ROBERT KYLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Need authorities under which the court can grant reformation of will. Sec. 21311 applies to Will Contest which petition affirms as not a will contest. a. Need authority under which the court can grant to establish a special needs trust. Sec.15409 applies to an existing trust, which is not a case in this matter. b. Need allegation on the beneficiary's qualification of a special needs trust and further allegation if the draft trust meets all requirements for Federal and State. c. Appears the remaining assets needs to be distributed to the beneficiary's heirs at law upon the beneficiary's passing even though petitioner may be the closet relative at this time. d. Appears a guardian ad litem may be need as the beneficiary's interest has not been represented. e. Appears government agencies may need to be noticed with a copy of petition as (and if) sec. 3600 et seq. are applicable - State Director of Health Care Services, the Director of State Hospitals, and the Director of Developmental Services. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EMMA CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 04/24/2023: 1) The caption of the proposed order must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) The supplemental declaration filed on 04/12/2023 is treated as an amendment to the petition requiring new notice to be given at least 15 days before a continued hearing. CRC 7.3(4) & 7.53(a). Note: Item 1 of the DE-120 form must specifically list the supplemental declaration as an amendment to the petition. 3) An allegation must be made as to whether there are outstanding bills due to a third party for preparing this account. LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(a)(iii). 4) An allegation must be made as to whether the estate cash has been kept in interest-bearing accounts. Probate Code section 1064(a)(5). 5) A proof must be filed to show that the estate cash on hand is fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is otherwise held in the form of investment(s) authorized by section 9730. 6) The filing fee for the spousal property petition was $435. 7) An original small estate affidavit signed by Denise Chew, together with a certified copy of Jane Leong's death certificate, must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). 8) An original small estate affidavit signed by Robert Yee, Eric Yee, and Debra Yee, together with a certified copy of Cobina Wyatt's death certificate, must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). 9) An original small estate affidavit signed by Valerie Smith, Sharon Chan, and Jeffrey Chan, together with a certified copy of Rosa Chan's death certificate, must be conventionally filed with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). 10) According to the second supplement filed on 09/16/2021, Cobina Wyatt was survived by her spouse. The issue of how distribution to and from Cobina Wyatt's postdeceased spouse can be made must be addressed. See section 11802. 11) According to the second supplement filed on 09/16/2021, Rosa Chan was survived by her spouse. The issue of how distribution to and from Rosa Chan's postdeceased spouse can be made must be addressed. See section 11802. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST AGREEMENT DATED NOVEMBER 19, 1976 CREATING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Approving Modification Of Trust'S Terms Under Probate Code Section 15403(B)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/25/23 to address the following issues: 1) Venue has not been addressed, including discussion of Probate Code 17002 and 17005. The Probate Code 16061.7 Notice provided by current trustee must also be filed. 2) Petition must state the names and last known addresses of all beneficiaries, vested or contingent [CRC 7.902] and those entitled to notice [Probate Code 17201]. 3) The captions cite Probate Code 15403(b), but no discussion is provided as to whether this petition meets the requirements under that code section. Supplemental declaration verified by the petitioner to be filed by 9/11/23.
